steps.text: Determine if the facility radiation protection program has been maintained in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee commitments, since the last inspection.
Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements, and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, 'Notice to Employees,' is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10 CFR 50.59, the license and Technical Specification (TS) requirements.
Determine if the licensee addressed radiologically significant events in accordance with regulatory and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Determine if an ALARA program has been implemented as required.
Determine if the licensee has developed, documented and implemented a radiation protection program in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Determine if the respiratory protection program meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if planned special exposures were made in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the dose to the embryo/fetus for declared pregnant women met regulatory requirements.
Use direct observation to verify inspection requirements if practicable.
Use records review, independent measurements, or discussions or walk-throughs with involved licensee personnel if direct observation is not practicable.
Review changes to the facility's radiation protection procedures.
Post Form NRC-3 conspicuously in appropriate areas.
Observe the postings of Form NRC-3 in one or two areas.
Observe controls for two radiologically controlled areas.
Post radiologically controlled areas conspicuously.
Control high radiation areas to prevent unauthorized access.
Evaluate the licensee’s use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors.
Observe the protective clothing worn by facility experimenters during work activities.
Discuss with individuals to determine their use of protective clothing if observation is not practical.
Instruct personnel in the proper use and function of the protective clothing.
Ensure provisions for the correct disposition of contaminated protective clothing.
Observe the personnel dosimetry worn by four facility workers.
Check if dosimetry devices are appropriate for the type and energy of the emitted radiation.
Instruct facility personnel in the proper use and function of dosimeters.
Check alarming dosimeters before each operation.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the controlled area and check if dosimeter readings are recorded.
Review the process of processing film or TLD badges.
Observe four facility workers leaving a controlled area.
Check for contamination of personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing before leaving a controlled area.
Use hand held friskers, stand-in monitors, portal monitors, or hand and foot monitors for exit monitoring.
Monitor hands with hand held friskers before removing the probe from its mounting.
Detect alpha contamination if suspected using appropriate methods.
Evaluate the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers and, where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe respective activities and discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals.
Determine if instruction appropriate to the tasks to be performed was provided.
Determine if there was adequate supervision of their activities.
Accompany a person qualified in the licensee’s radiation control techniques on normal rounds.
Request to be shown the manner in which radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities are normally accomplished.
Perform operability checks on all instrumentation before use.
Examine the licensee’s training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Determine whether surveys were performed as required by the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
Check for excessive levels of contamination within restricted areas and at the exits from the restricted area.
Perform independent radiological surveys as needed to verify licensee assumptions or measurements.
Compare measurements taken during surveys with those completed by the licensee in the same areas.
Review surveys conducted by the licensee during both shutdown and operations following changes in shielding.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee’s radiation surveys.
Review 50 percent of the facility’s personnel dosimetry record.
Determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee’s ALARA program.
Determine if the licensee reported the over exposures in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.2202 and 20.2203.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.
Review any organizational change involving the radiation protection manager.
Check if personnel who oversee the radiation protection program are qualified by training and experience to perform intended functions.
Review major radiation control events at the facility since the last inspection.
Determine if major incidents were reported to the NRC in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2202.
Review if corrective actions were reviewed by the safety review committee and consistent with facility TS and procedures.

preconditions.text: If radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area, evaluate personnel occupancy of these areas and the potential dose to occupants.
If contamination levels exceed double the background, a barrier should be set unless the contamination is removable and the area is immediately decontaminated.

constraints.text: Require a licensee escort trained in radiation protection before entering a high radiation area.
Personal protective equipment may include gloves, booties, rubber boots, hair covers, lab coats, coveralls, and rubber or plastic aprons.
Some articles may need to be held for radioactive decay before disposal.
Monitoring devices should meet the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501.
An appropriate check of contamination is required before leaving a controlled area in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart F.
